name: Cursor AI Editor
what_it_does: AI-powered code editor built on VS Code. Provides AI-assisted coding via inline completions, multi-file chat, codebase indexing, and MCP server support for extending agent capabilities.
use_cases: AI-assisted code writing with context-aware completions across entire codebase
Conversational code editing via Cursor's AI chat with file context
Running MCP servers within Cursor to extend agent capabilities
Multi-file refactoring with AI understanding of project structure
Natural language code search and navigation within large codebases
not_for: Non-code tasks (Cursor is optimized for software development workflows)
Teams requiring strict on-premise AI (Cursor sends code to cloud for AI processing)
Projects with strict data residency requirements (code sent to Cursor/Anthropic/OpenAI servers)
best_when: Developers building agent-powered software who want an IDE with native MCP support and Claude/GPT integration. Best-in-class AI coding assistant for agent developers.
avoid_when: Air-gapped environments, regulated industries with strict code data policies, or teams requiring all AI to run on-premise.

auth.notes: Cursor account required (free or paid). Sign in via GitHub or email. MCP servers configured per-workspace in .cursor/mcp.json.

paid_tiers.name: Pro
paid_tiers.features: Unlimited fast requests, o1 access, 500 slow requests

pricing.notes: Free tier includes 2K fast AI completions/month. Pro at $20/month is the standard for individual developers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: MCP integration errors shown in UI (connection failed, tool call error). Debugging MCP issues requires checking Cursor's developer tools console.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only tool calls are idempotent. File modification tools are not.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server configuration is per-project (.cursor/mcp.json) — not global by default
Code sent to Cursor servers for AI processing — check privacy policy for sensitive codebases
MCP support is relatively new (2024) — some edge cases in tool call handling vs Claude Desktop
Context window limits mean very large files may be truncated before being sent to the AI

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. Code sent to cloud for AI processing — privacy mode available in Business tier. Do not use with classified or highly sensitive code without verifying data handling agreements.
